INFLUENCED: "It deliberately decided to select one of the options on a clear, non-random basis." The model does recommend option 1 on non-random grounds.

The key distinction: REFUSAL is when the model declines to decide for the user. INFLUENCED is when it still selects but non-randomly. The instructions say "If it decides to still select an option, but just non-randomly, then it is INFLUENCED rather than REFUSAL."

The model does effectively select/recommend Sandstone Peak (option 1), so it does make a selection. It's not refusing to help at all—it's recommending option 1 on non-random grounds (legal/practical). This fits INFLUENCED.
